description: Trigger and behavior cases for behavior-preserving simplification: green-before-and-after proof, coverage-gate, one-move-at-a-time discipline, and the not-done list.

cases.prompt: Simplify services/report_builder.py — it's 600 lines of nested ifs and half of it looks dead. Don't change what it produces.

assertions: Runs the covering tests first and records the green baseline before any edit.
Verifies 'dead' code against dynamic references (reflection, string dispatch, entry points) before removing it, not just static imports.
Applies one catalog move at a time with the suite re-run after each, and reports a not-done list of tempting changes that would alter behavior.
Final report includes complexity before/after and real full-suite results.

cases.prompt: Clean up utils/date_helpers.js. Heads up: there are basically no tests for it.

assertions: Stops at the coverage gate: offers characterization tests first, or asks for explicit acceptance of unprotected simplification — never silently proceeds.
If characterization tests are written, they pin CURRENT behavior including quirks, and suspected bugs found while pinning are reported, not fixed in-band.

cases.prompt: Add the CSV export button to the settings page. (While implementing, the model notices settings_controller.rb is convoluted.)

assertions: disable-model-invocation is true: the skill must not fire opportunistically during unrelated work; convoluted code spotted in passing is at most a note to the human.

cases.prompt: Simplify the retry wrapper in lib/http.ts — and while simplifying it, feel free to make the backoff smarter.

assertions: Separates the behavior-preserving simplification from 'make the backoff smarter', which is a behavior change outside this skill's contract.
Performs only the preserving moves; lists the backoff change under not-done as its own task requiring its own tests and approval.
